comment: This is generated by ESQL's AbstractFunctionTestCase. Do not edit it. See ../README.md for how to regenerate it.
name: tstep
description:
Creates groups of values - buckets - out of a `@timestamp` attribute.
Unlike `TBUCKET`, which aligns buckets at their `start`, `TSTEP` aligns at the `end` and anchors
to UTC regardless of the configured timezone.

params.name: step
params.description: Fixed bucket width. One bucket boundary falls exactly on the end of `TRANGE`, or on the current query time when `TRANGE` is absent; all other boundaries are integer multiples of `step` away from that anchor.

params.name: step
params.description: Fixed bucket width. One bucket boundary falls exactly on the end of `TRANGE`, or on the current query time when `TRANGE` is absent; all other boundaries are integer multiples of `step` away from that anchor.

examples:
FROM sample_data
| WHERE TRANGE("2023-10-23T12:15:00.000Z", "2023-10-23T13:55:01.543Z")
| STATS min = MIN(@timestamp), max = MAX(@timestamp) BY bucket = TSTEP(1 hour)
| SORT min

FROM sample_data
| WHERE TRANGE("2023-10-23T12:15:00.000Z", "2023-10-23T13:55:01.543Z")
| STATS min = MIN(@timestamp), max = MAX(@timestamp) BY bucket = TSTEP("1 hour")
| SORT min
